name: Water and Power Community
description_short: Water and Power Community Credit Union (WPCCU) is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union celebrating 90 years of service. They offer auto loans, mortgages, credit cards, certificates, and retirement planning.
description_long:
Water and Power Community Credit Union has been serving its members for 90 years, as evidenced by their current 90th Anniversary celebration campaign. The organization operates as a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution, consistent with the credit union model regulated by the NCUA. Based on their website positioning and service offerings, WPCCU appears to serve employees in the water and power utility sectors, though membership details are not fully specified in available content.

WPCCU provides a comprehensive suite of consumer financial products including auto loans (APR as low as 4.74%), home loans and HELOCs (rates as low as 7.75% APR), credit cards, certificates of deposit with competitive rates (APY up to 3.90%), IRAs, and a proprietary "Power Cash Loan" product. They also offer investment advisory services through partnership with LPL Financial for retirement planning, and have recently added a service allowing members to make loan payments from other institutions without logging into their online banking platform.

WPCCU distinguishes itself through active community engagement, including sponsorships and volunteer efforts supporting local veterans as of November 2025. They emphasize member education through 2026 financial webinars and financial planning assistance. The organization maintains robust fraud protection awareness, actively warning members about scams and providing fraud reporting mechanisms. Their digital accessibility commitment is stated explicitly on their website with high-contrast viewing options for visually impaired users.

As a credit union, WPCCU members benefit from the not-for-profit structure and member-ownership model, though the website provides limited transparency on membership eligibility requirements, fee structures, or specific dividend policies. Current promotional rates appear competitive, but standard terms, APRs for less-qualified borrowers, and membership cost details are not disclosed in available web content. The organization appears well-established and legitimate based on professional website presentation and community involvement documentation.
services: Auto loans with rates from 4.74% APR
Home Equity Lines of Credit (HELOC) from 7.75% APR
Certificates of Deposit up to 3.90% APY
Investment advisory services through LPL Financial
Fraud reporting and fraud alert services
pros: 90-year establishment history demonstrating long-term stability and member commitment
Competitive auto loan rates starting at 4.74% APR and certificates up to 3.90% APY
HELOC products available with rates as low as 7.75% APR for home equity access
Partnership with LPL Financial for professional investment advisory and retirement planning services
Member education through 2026 financial webinars focused on financial goal planning
Cross-institution loan payment capability without requiring login to online banking
Active community involvement with local veterans sponsorships and volunteer initiatives
cons: Website does not clearly specify membership eligibility requirements or how to qualify
No fee schedule, annual membership costs, or dividend information disclosed online
Limited information about minimum deposit requirements beyond the $1,000 minimum for 90th Anniversary certificates
No transparent comparison of rates for borrowers with fair or poor credit scores
Missing details about loan approval timelines, processing fees, or prepayment penalties
best_for: Water and power utility sector employees seeking member-owned financial services with competitive rates
Members prioritizing community-focused banking with emphasis on local veteran support
Borrowers seeking comprehensive financial planning through integrated investment advisory services
Consumers interested in certificate products and retirement accounts with professional guidance
diagnosis: WPCCU is best for members of water and power utility sectors or those eligible for membership seeking stable, community-focused credit union banking with competitive consumer loan rates and integrated investment planning services. Primary caveat: membership eligibility, fees, and full rate schedules are not transparent on their website, requiring direct contact to confirm qualification and complete cost comparison.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Water and Power Community (LOS ANGELES, CA) | CreditDoc
meta_description: Water and Power Community — NCUA-insured credit union in LOS ANGELES, CA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
